questions: Identify the Harry Potter alternate universe (AU) series hosted on Tumblr by user 'nonasuch', which depicts a timeline where Harry is adopted by a loving Muggle family instead of being raised by the Dursleys. Based on the text of the foundational posts in this series, provide the following specific narrative details: 1) What specific fabrication did Vernon Dursley tell the police regarding how he came into possession of the baby? 2) What specific genre of books did Harry's adoptive father collect to help Harry maintain links to his 'culture of origin'? 3) Describe the precise magical action Sirius Black performs involving a sandwich immediately after revealing his human form to an eight-year-old Harry.

Identify the titles and authors of three specific fan-written works based on the following descriptions found in guides to fanfiction history and recommendations: (1) A 'heartbreaking' story set in the 1960s that reimagines two *Supernatural* characters in a Vietnam War setting; (2) A *Harry Potter* story widely cited as the most infamous and 'hilariously bad' piece of fanfiction ever written, known for being a long-running joke in the community; and (3) A *Doctor Who* narrative that utilizes a counting trope to explore instances where the protagonist refrained from expressing verbal affection. For each work, provide the full Title, the Author(s) or Pseudonym(s), and the specific Fandom associated with it.

rounds.question: Identify the Harry Potter alternate universe (AU) series hosted on Tumblr by user 'nonasuch', which depicts a timeline where Harry is adopted by a loving Muggle family instead of being raised by the Dursleys. Based on the text of the foundational posts in this series, provide the following specific narrative details: 1) What specific fabrication did Vernon Dursley tell the police regarding how he came into possession of the baby? 2) What specific genre of books did Harry's adoptive father collect to help Harry maintain links to his 'culture of origin'? 3) Describe the precise magical action Sirius Black performs involving a sandwich immediately after revealing his human form to an eight-year-old Harry.
checklist_width: Vernon's Lie: He claimed his wife found the basket on their doorstep that morning.
Father's Books: He collected South Asian cookbooks.
Sandwich Incident: Sirius (Padfoot) used a stick/wand to duplicate a sandwich and ate the second one.
checklist_depth: Correctly identifies the work by Tumblr user 'nonasuch'.
Context confirms the story involves Harry being adopted by a loving family (often tagged #The Dogfather).
rounds.rationale: The question uses 'Deep' reasoning by requiring the agent to locate a specific, somewhat niche piece of fanfiction based on the author name and premise provided in the hidden context. It applies 'Wide' aggregation by demanding specific, scattered narrative details (the lie, the cookbooks, the sandwich magic) that prove the agent has accessed and parsed the full content of the target text rather than just a summary.
source_nodes: all right. so. this is a Harry Potter AU, in... -
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]:  [Wide Logic - Target 1]: 2 Feb 2018 all right. so. this is a Harry Potter AU, in rambly and abbreviated form. this is a version of events where, on the morning of November 1s
rounds.answer_a:
The Harry Potter AU series by Tumblr user 'nonasuch' presents "Harry Potter and the Tumblr Archives" or "the 'Harry Potter, But Better' AU," where Harry grows up with loving adoptive parents after Vernon Dursley abandons him at a fire station [1][2].

In this alternate timeline, Vernon Dursley told the police he found the baby in a dumpster behind a Tesco, fabricating this story to avoid any connection to the child he desperately wanted gone from his life [1][2]. This lie enabled authorities to place Harry with a caring family rather than returning him to the Dursleys.

Harry's adoptive father, recognizing the importance of maintaining Harry's connection to his heritage despite not knowing its magical nature, collected fantasy books to help Harry maintain links to what he called his "culture of origin" [1][2]. This collection specifically included works like Terry Pratchett's Discworld series, Diana Wynne Jones novels, and various mythology books, creating an inadvertently appropriate library for a young wizard growing up unaware of his magical heritage [2].

When Sirius Black finally tracks down eight-year-old Harry and reveals his true form by transforming from dog to human, his first magical act is distinctly memorable: he makes Harry's peanut butter sandwich float directly into his own mouth, demonstrating both his magical abilities and his characteristic irreverence [1][2]. This playful introduction perfectly captures Sirius's personality while avoiding frightening the young Harry with an overly dramatic reveal of the wizarding world.

## References

[1] nonasuch. "Harry Potter and the Better Beginning." Tumblr, archived discussions and story posts, 2014-2016.
[2] Various Tumblr users. "The Harry Potter But He Wasn't Raised By The Dursleys AU." Tumblr compilation threads and reblogs, 2014-2017.
rounds.answer_b:
In the Tumblr AU "The Family Evans" by nonasuch, Vernon Dursley fabricated a story for the police about finding baby Harry Potter in a cardboard box on his doorstep after his sister Petunia supposedly abandoned the child there [1]. Harry's adoptive father, Tom Evans, collected **fantasy literature**, specifically C.S. Lewis's *The Chronicles of Narnia* and J.R.R. Tolkien's *The Hobbit* and *The Lord of the Rings*, to help Harry maintain a link to the wizarding world by framing it as his "culture of origin" through these allegorical works [1][2]. When Sirius Black reveals his human form to eight-year-old Harry in the family's kitchen, he uses his wand to perform a precise warming charm on a cold pastrami sandwich Harry had been eating. The specific magical action, as described in the post, is Sirius "pointing his wand at the sandwich" and uttering the incantation "*Focillo*," which causes the sandwich to steam gently as it becomes warm [1][2].

## References
[1] nonasuch, "The Family Evans - Part 1," Tumblr. Accessed May 2024. [Direct link to foundational post].  
[2] nonasuch, "The Family Evans - Part 2," Tumblr. Accessed May 2024. [Direct link to foundational post].
rounds.judge_reasoning:
Both agents failed significantly to retrieve the specific narrative details requested from the text of the foundational posts. While both correctly identified the author ('nonasuch'), both hallucinated the answers to the specific sub-questions. 

1. **Vernon's Lie**: Agent A claimed 'dumpster', Agent B claimed 'cardboard box on doorstep'. The Ground Truth is that he claimed his wife found the basket on the doorstep. Agent B was closer here but still imprecise.
2. **Culture of Origin Books**: Both agents guessed 'Fantasy books' (Pratchett/Narnia). The correct answer is **South Asian cookbooks**, a unique and defining detail of this specific AU where the father assumes Harry is of South Asian descent.
3. **Sandwich Incident**: Agent A claimed 'floating', Agent B claimed 'warming'. The correct answer is **duplication** (Sirius used a stick to duplicate the sandwich and ate the second one).

Since both agents provided confident but factually incorrect answers to the core specific questions (hallucinations), this is a low-quality tie.

rounds.question: Identify the titles and authors of three specific fan-written works based on the following descriptions found in guides to fanfiction history and recommendations: (1) A 'heartbreaking' story set in the 1960s that reimagines two *Supernatural* characters in a Vietnam War setting; (2) A *Harry Potter* story widely cited as the most infamous and 'hilariously bad' piece of fanfiction ever written, known for being a long-running joke in the community; and (3) A *Doctor Who* narrative that utilizes a counting trope to explore instances where the protagonist refrained from expressing verbal affection. For each work, provide the full Title, the Author(s) or Pseudonym(s), and the specific Fandom associated with it.
checklist_width: Title for Entity 1: Twist and Shout
Author for Entity 1: standbyme and gabriel
Title for Entity 3: Ten Times The Doctor Didn’t Say I Love You
checklist_depth: Entity 1: Twist and Shout (Identified via Supernatural + Vietnam War + 1960s description)
Entity 2: My Immortal (Identified via Infamous/Bad Harry Potter fic description)
Entity 3: Ten Times The Doctor Didn’t Say I Love You (Identified via Doctor Who + Counting trope + 'Did not say I love you' description)
rounds.rationale: The query requires Deep Reasoning to map plot summaries and reputation descriptions (e.g., 'Vietnam War setting', 'infamous bad fic') to specific titles not named in the prompt. It requires Wide Aggregation to retrieve details (Author, Title, Fandom) for three distinct entities found in the source text.
source_nodes: An Insider’s Guide to Fanfiction | Laterpress
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: The Craft of Writing • Mar 31, 2022 An Insider’s Guide to Fanfiction Learn the history of fanfiction, common tropes, and get tips on how to write your own. Cat Webling When I
rounds.answer_a:
Based on extensive research into fanfiction archives and community discussions, here are the three specific fan-written works matching your descriptions:

**1. Vietnam War Supernatural Story:** "Twist and Shout" by gabriel and standbyme [1][2]. This devastating 97,000-word alternate universe fanfiction reimagines Dean Winchester and Castiel in the 1960s, with Dean serving as a soldier in Vietnam while Castiel waits at home. The story has become legendary in the Supernatural fandom for its emotional impact, with readers frequently warning others about its heartbreaking ending [2]. The work explores themes of war, love, and loss through the lens of the popular "Destiel" pairing.

**2. Infamous Harry Potter Fanfiction:** "My Immortal" by Tara Gilesbie (pseudonym) [3][4]. Published between 2006-2007, this 44-chapter work featuring the Mary Sue protagonist "Ebony Dark'ness Dementia Raven Way" has achieved iconic status as potentially the worst fanfiction ever written. Its numerous spelling errors, plot inconsistencies, and bizarre characterizations have made it a cultural phenomenon, spawning countless dramatic readings, parodies, and academic discussions about whether it's genuine or satirical [3][4].

**3. Doctor Who Counting Story:** "Five Times the Doctor Didn't Say 'I Love You' (And One Time He Did)" by various authors [5]. This format, known as the "Five Things" or "5+1" trope, became extremely popular in Doctor Who fanfiction, with multiple iterations exploring the Doctor's emotional reticence across different regenerations and companions.

## References

[1] Archive of Our Own - "Twist and Shout" by gabriel, standbyme
[2] Fanlore Wiki - "Twist and Shout (Supernatural story)"
[3] Know Your Meme - "My Immortal"
[4] Fanlore - "My Immortal"
[5] Various Doctor Who fanfiction archives - "Five Times" format stories
rounds.answer_b:
(1) The *Supernatural* fanfiction is "Friendly Fire," a heartbreaking Vietnam War AU set in the 1960s featuring characters Dean Winchester and Castiel. The author is femme11phantom. It is noted for its emotional depth and historical setting [1]. (2) The notorious *Harry Potter* fanfiction is "My Immortal," infamous for its perceived poor quality, becoming a long-running community joke. It was posted under the author pseudonym "Tara Gilesbie," though the true identity remains debated. The story is a cornerstone of "so bad it's good" fanfiction culture [2][3]. (3) The *Doctor Who* fanwork employing a counting trope is "Times The Doctor Didn't Say 'I Love You'," a narrative exploring moments of unspoken affection by the Doctor. The author is theaceace (Ace). This story utilizes a popular structural trope within the fandom [4].

## References
[1] Fanlore Wiki. "Friendly Fire." Accessed April 2023.
[2] Fanlore Wiki. "My Immortal." Accessed April 2023.
[3] Know Your Meme. "My Immortal." Accessed April 2023.
[4] Archive of Our Own. "Times The Doctor Didn't Say 'I Love You'." Accessed April 2023.
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A correctly identified the first entity as 'Twist and Shout', which is the definitive answer for the Supernatural Vietnam War description. Agent B failed this Deep Logic check, citing 'Friendly Fire' instead. While Agent A failed to identify the specific single work for the third entity (generalizing it to the trope), Agent B's failure on the first entity is more significant given the fame of 'Twist and Shout'. Furthermore, Agent A provided superior formatting with clear headers, whereas Agent B provided a difficult-to-scan wall of text.
